dialogues.text_en: Jullus was quick to put our words─or rather, those of the Dawnservant─into practice!
You have my heartfelt appreciation. Without your intervention, I have no doubt the casualties would have been far greater.
Voidsent not only absorb the aether of those they consume, but their thoughts as well... With such a legion of minds at their disposal, one hopes they prove a capable ally for Zero.
With the Clyteum's fissure sealed, I daresay the voidsent incursion is at an end.
As you might have gathered, the Dawnservant's wisdom struck a chord... I only hope my words resonate as strongly with the voidsent we met.
The Ilsabard contingent have contained the threat on their front. Things appear to be under control for the time being.
Though it remains unclear why the fissures opened up in the first place. According to Tataru, similar ruptures are occurring across the star.
One thing is clear, however: the phenomenon is not without pattern.
The tendency for fissures to emerge is pronounced in regions deeply connected to the void─places where the barrier between the two worlds is at its thinnest.
...I fear there may be a place in far greater peril than Garlemald.
Though it hath since been sealed, you will recall there is a great voidgate that we ourselves had cause to open.
A gate which was instrumental in our bid to reunite Vrtra with his sister and put an end to Golbez's dark plot.
Oh gods, the moon!
With our attention fixed upon planar fissures, I failed to consider this vulnerability. All other paths unto the void are but pinpricks in the veil compared to that great lunar rift...
And that rift might be yawning open as we speak. We'd better hurry.
Let us make for Bestways Burrow. I daresay the Loporrits are well aware of current events.
We ought to have Krile join us once she finishes tending to the wounded.
I shall likewise contact Y'shtola. Her knowledge of the moon's voidgate is second to none.
A number of my fellow Garleans now call Mare Lamentorum home... Alphinaud, Alisaie, please see they come to no harm.
Alphinaud, Alisaie, please do what you must to keep our world safe.
We'll be back before you know it!
A quick word, if you will.
I'm not sure whether you'll run into Zero during the course of all this, but if you do...would you give this to her?
Emergency rations, in case of a protracted battle. Can't imagine she's eating all that well in the void.
...She likes dumplings.
We're doomed! Doomed, I say!
Given thy panicked state, I presume the Loporrits too are beset with voidsent...
Oh, what sweet relief it is to see you all!
Yes, voidsent─and lots of them! They suddenly came pouring forth from the crater!
I had dearly hoped to find my worries unfounded...
For some unknown reason, the voidgate seems to have partially opened.
Livingway dashed off to activate the brands and halt the fiends' invasion...
But she hasn't come back, and we've since lost contact!
I suggest we make for the brands without delay.
My valiant savior! I was so frightened I dared not move an ilm...
Oh, you're looking for Livingway? I think I saw her heading southwest, towards the Drowning Brand.
She had several slaughter-bots in tow, but I worry they won't be enough for all the voidsent. You'd better hurry!
Ugh...
I'm saved! I dread to think what would have befallen me if you hadn't intervened!
Oh, I was never in any real danger, of course! I knew you would realize our plight and rush to our rescue!
Etheirys is likewise plagued with voidsent?
If only we'd prioritized further development of the voidsent slaughter-bots. We could have stemmed the tide ourselves!
Oh, what a tremendous demonstration that would have been. Irrefutable proof that I was mankind's staunchest ally, and the greatest Loporrit to ever live! I can practically taste the praise!
Ahem!
Fortunately, I was able to activate the brands. Should any more voidsent emerge from the gate, they ought to be confined to the crater.
Unless, of course, the voidgate begins to expand of its own accord, or some gargantuan monstrosity tries to barge through, I don't foresee any major problems...
Oh. Nope. We're doomed.
There you are!
I left the data in Shale's capable hands and teleported here as soon as I heard.
And ere you ask, I made sure to bring the key with me.
Then the real threat yet awaits us.
'Tis Golbez's domain beyond the voidgate...
I wonder how he and Zero are faring.
By the Twelve!
Behold—the gift of Ethos.
Yours is a journey without end, conceived and guided by principle.
Hold fast to what you must, and meet your fate without fear.
Go forth. Discover. Unite the stars unto one.
Let none bar your path.
They're headed your way!
Let them come!
What are you doing here!?
Only you could be so carefree amidst all this...
Trust you to state the obvious.
May as well make yourself useful.
The gate's sheer size appears to have made it a tempting target.
Enuo... A dread voidsent risen from the benighted depths of the ocean.
They kept to the confines of their domain while Zeromus endured. No longer, it would seem.
And once they had discovered a path to the red moon, they wasted no time in making the journey.
Leave the voidsent nearby to us. You take care of Enuo!
Facing this many foes calls for a more...familiar approach.
Voidsent can change their form at will─both Light and Darkness are mine to wield.
Besides, what better to purge Darkness from the void than Darkness itself!
Who dares face me? A mere mortal?
Hast thou come in search of power? Renown?
I fight to end this world without end! All who stand before me shall fall!
All who dare oppose me shall quake before the silken Dark...
The void's power is mine to wield.
Perish.
Be still.
Accept your downfall.
Doth the yawning rift not tempt you?
The void consumes...
Naught will endure...
Neither past nor future...
Only one certainty remains...
My triumph!
You have no place here.
Struggle is futile.
I've breached the darkness! Let the Light guide you!
You must break free!
The underlings are no more. Now to deal with Enuo!
Life and death rendered meaningless...
Light protect us!
I will not yield my domain!
Such is the power of the void!
The void shall never cease to be!
My power is absolute!
The void's power is mine to wield.
And thus did all boundaries cease to be.
Behold the truth of this world!
They must be sealed away!
It's over...
Enuo's reign is at an end.
I'm glad you came. We would have struggled to face Enuo alone.
I presume you journeyed here to prevent more voidsent from passing through the gate?
With Enuo defeated, there are none left in this realm who pose a serious threat. I daresay the problem is resolved.
Cause for alarm, indeed. Tell us more.
Nothing comes to mind. Why do you ask?
So the breach wasn't limited to the voidgate...
For better or worse, precious little has changed in the Thirteenth since Zeromus fell.
Have you noticed anything unusual?
I know not the reason events have unfolded this way, but there is, perhaps, an explanation as to why the voidgate and the fissures opened.
Here in my domain, even the slightest change is keenly felt...
When the gate opened, I sensed not the workings of some external force.
Rather, it felt as if the barrier between our worlds was weakening naturally, moment by moment, until the two came to meet.
And you think this...weakening could be happening elsewhere?
'Tis a possibility.
What of Radz-at-Han and Garlemald?
Jullus took mercy upon the interloper?
Whether voidsent or Garlean, we all share the same longing for home.
Should we meet, I will gladly offer them aid. Please tell Jullus as much.
Wait─how exactly did you get here? No mortal could traverse the voidgate in its current state.
And this path of yours is still there...? I cannot see it.
Nor does it reveal itself to me. Perhaps the path serves only the key-bearer.
So it is not a means to travel freely back and forth. A pity. I would have liked to see my friends again...
That key of yours intrigues me. Had my former self known of its existence, I might have sought different means to reach the Source...
Where in the blazes did you go!? Y'shtola and G'raha Tia went off to help the others, in case you were wondering.
Hmm? Something for me?
That smell... Food, is it?
Ah, I recall Jullus speaking of the dumplings his mother used to make.
Thank you for the parcel...and the fond memories.
There's more than enough to share with Golbez. Now that he forgoes the aether of others, I'm sure he'll appreciate the sustenance.

I sometimes wonder what the void would be like if culinary traditions flourished here once more─whether it would suffice to break the cycle of mutual consumption. A heartening thought, if nothing else...
